query: pulpotomy purposes in young permanent teeth

retrieved_docs.title: Tooth Fracture -- Treatment / Management -- Partial pulpotomy
retrieved_docs.content: Pulp testing and radiographic evaluation are recommended during follow-up visits at 6 to 8 weeks, 3 months, 5 months, and 1 year. Each follow-up appointment should include a clinical and radiographic assessment to evaluate pulp vitality and identify any complications.

retrieved_docs.title: Tooth Fracture -- Treatment / Management -- Partial pulpotomy
retrieved_docs.content: Partial pulpotomy involves amputating the pulp 2 mm below the exposure site, where the tissue is presumed to be healthy. [16] This technique is the treatment of choice for young teeth with open apices when pulp capping is contraindicated, such as when excessive time has elapsed since the trauma or when the exposure diameter is too large. Partial pulpotomy serves as a provisional yet long-term approach, allowing the tooth to remain vital for as long as possible. However, root canal treatment followed by appropriate restoration may eventually be required. Restorative options include freehand or indirect composite resin restoration, fragment reattachment if available, and ceramic restoration.

retrieved_docs.title: Avulsed Tooth -- Treatment / Management -- Follow up Appointment
retrieved_docs.content: Immature teeth where the root has not yet wholly formed have a greater chance of revascularization after soaking in doxycycline. Primary teeth should not undergo replantation as this can damage the underlying permanent tooth germ.

retrieved_docs.title: Trauma to the Primary Dentition -- Treatment / Management -- Crown-Root Fractures
retrieved_docs.content: If the tooth is unrestorable, extract all loose fragments taking care not to damage the permanent successor, leave any firm root fragment in situ, or extract the entire tooth. When the tooth has been retained, a follow-up clinical examination is recommended after one week, six to eight weeks, and one year. A radiographic examination should be done one year following pulpotomy or root canal treatment.

retrieved_docs.title: Trauma to the Primary Dentition -- Treatment / Management -- Crown-Root Fractures
retrieved_docs.content: The immediate management is to remove the loose fragment, often under local anesthesia, and assess if the crown can be restored. If the tooth is restorable and has no pulpal exposure, cover the exposed dentine with glass ionomer. If the tooth is restorable, but the pulp has been exposed, perform a pulpotomy or root canal treatment depending on the root development stage and fracture level.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Emergencies -- Treatment / Management -- Tooth Fractures
retrieved_docs.content: Partial pulpotomy is indicated when the duration of pulp exposure has been extensive, or the diameter of exposure exceeds 1.5 mm. In these cases, a partial pulpotomy can be performed, ultimately allowing the tooth to remain vital for an extended time. However, the tooth eventually requires root canal therapy and restoration. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Extraction of Teeth -- Indications -- Pulpal/Apical Pathology
retrieved_docs.content: A common sequela of dental caries is pulpal and periapical pathology. A dental extraction would be indicated for a tooth with pulpal, or apical pathology that cannot be resolved with endodontic treatment or that would not be restorable following the endodontic treatment. In some cases, a dental extraction is indicated when the patient chooses not to have endodontic treatment but would still like the tooth to be treated.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Emergencies -- Treatment / Management -- Tooth Fractures
retrieved_docs.content: Pulp capping works best in immature teeth with open apices, where the pulp was considered healthy before the trauma. Indications for pulp capping include pulp exposure of a small diameter (less than 1.5 mm) that has only been exposed for a short time, thus minimizing the risk of bacterial invasion. [48]

retrieved_docs.title: Trauma to the Primary Dentition -- Treatment / Management -- Crown Fractures With Exposed Pulp (complicated crown fracture)
retrieved_docs.content: The treatment aims at preserving the pulp by partial pulpotomy. Local anesthesia is required, and a non-setting calcium hydroxide paste is applied over the pulp and covered with glass-ionomer and composite. For more extensive pulpal exposures, a cervical pulpotomy is indicated. Follow-up clinical examination is recommended after one week, six to eight weeks, and one year, with a radiographic examination at one year following pulpotomy.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Materials: Biodentine, a Calcium Silicate Bioactive -- Clinical Significance -- Clinical Applications of Biodentine: Pulpotomy in Permanent Teeth with Symptoms of Irreversible Pulpitis
retrieved_docs.content: A study on the outcomes of Biodentine full pulpotomy in adult permanent teeth with carious exposure and clinical signs of irreversible pulpitis has been conducted. The study's results highlight this pulpotomy approach's success, especially when patients were selected based on the time needed to achieve hemostasis rather than solely relying on clinical symptoms. A full pulpotomy removes the entire coronal pulp while maintaining the health of the remaining radicular portion, increasing the chances of eliminating infected or irreversibly inflamed tissue. The ability to control bleeding after the amputation of the infected pulp tissue is a marker for the degree of inflammation and the potential for healing the remaining pulp tissue, influencing the choice between partial and complete pulpotomy. [6] While Biodentine has shown promise in previous clinical trials for pulpotomy, this study explicitly assesses its use in adult patients with symptomatic irreversible pulpitis.

retrieved_docs.title: Avulsed Tooth -- Treatment / Management -- Follow up Appointment
retrieved_docs.content: The splint must be removed after two weeks, where the tooth will be clinically and radiographically examined. After removing the splint, the tooth’s mobility is checked, and a pulp vitality test should be performed, preferably pulse oximetry or electric testing. [15]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Materials: Biodentine, a Calcium Silicate Bioactive -- Clinical Significance -- Clinical Applications of Biodentine: Primary Teeth Pulpotomy
retrieved_docs.content: Pulpotomy is a procedure to preserve the function and vitality of the radicular pulp by surgically removing the coronal part of an exposed vital pulp. Formocresol has traditionally been used because of its clinical success and ease of use, but concern has been raised regarding mutagenic, toxic, and carcinogenic risks. Biodentine has been suggested as an alternative material to formocresol for pulpotomy procedures. A 2019 randomized clinical trial evaluated the use of Biodentine for pulpotomy in primary molars in children, comparing its clinical and radiographic success rates with formocresol. [5] Formocresol was chosen due to its long-term clinical success despite concerns over adverse reactions. MTA, another alternative, presented challenges in handling and setting time.

retrieved_docs.title: Tooth Fracture -- Treatment / Management -- Pulp treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Pulp therapy techniques aim to protect the exposed or traumatized dental pulp, with the primary goal of preserving tooth vitality. The choice of treatment depends on several factors, including the duration and diameter of exposure, the pulp's condition before the injury, the tooth’s age, the stage of root development, and the presence of simultaneous luxation injuries.

retrieved_docs.title: Trauma to the Primary Dentition -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Favorable outcomes of pulp healing include a normal color of the crown or transient red/grey or yellow discoloration and pulp canal obliteration. There will be no signs of pulp necrosis and infection, and in immature teeth, root development will continue. Additional favorable outcomes include undisturbed development and eruption of the permanent successor teeth.

retrieved_docs.title: Avulsed Tooth -- Treatment / Management -- Follow up Appointment
retrieved_docs.content: If the tooth is non-vital, a root canal treatment is indicated since there is no probability of revascularisation. [9] If the pulp is vital, an x-ray should be taken using an individualized holder. During the consecutive follow-up appointments (after one, three, and six months), a new x-ray will be taken to check for resorption. After this point, if there is no visible resorption, the patient is recalled every year for follow-up. In case of resorption, a root canal treatment is indicated. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Tooth Fracture -- Treatment / Management -- Pulp capping
retrieved_docs.content: Also known as direct pulp protection, pulp capping involves applying a material with regenerative properties, commonly calcium hydroxide, to small pulp exposures. This technique is indicated when the pulp has been exposed for a short duration, as prolonged exposure increases the risk of bacterial invasion and irreversible inflammation. [14] Clinically, the exposure diameter should not exceed 1.5 mm. The likelihood of success is greater in teeth with healthy pulp before the trauma, immature teeth with open apices, younger teeth, and fractures without concomitant luxation injuries. [15]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 250
retrieved_docs.content: The extraction of a single tooth is a direct dental procedure performed on the zuby (teeth), involving the removal of one tooth from the oral cavity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 244
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is classified as a dental procedure, indicating that it has a relationship with the broader category of dental procedures. This connection underscores that the act of extracting a single tooth is a specific type of dental intervention within the overall field of dental procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 226
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are directly performed on zuby mléčné, indicating that primary (baby) teeth serve as the specific sites for these dental interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 249
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are performed directly on zuby mléčné (the primary or “milk” teeth), indicating that therapeutic dental interventions often target the primary dentition. Likewise, disorders of teeth and jaw are located in zuby mléčné, showing that many oral pathologies also affect these early‑life teeth. Together, these relationships emphasize that both clinical treatments and disease processes in pediatric dentistry are closely linked to the primary teeth, underscoring the importance of careful assessment and management of zuby mléčné in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 0 -- Community 19
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a dental procedure that directly targets teeth (zuby) and is recognized as part of the broader set of dental procedures. Dental procedures themselves are also performed directly on deciduous teeth (zuby mléčné). Disorders of teeth and jaw are categorized under the parent concept of teeth (zuby) and are specifically located in the deciduous dentition (zuby mléčné). Consequently, both the extraction of a single tooth and other dental procedures may be applied to permanent and baby teeth, while disorders of the teeth and jaw primarily affect the baby teeth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 225
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a direct procedure performed on zuby (teeth), indicating that the teeth are the specific anatomical site for this dental intervention. Disorders of teeth and jaw are described as having zuby as their parent category, meaning that conditions affecting the teeth and jaw are organized under the broader classification of zuby. Consequently, zuby functions both as the procedural target for single‑tooth extraction and as the foundational element within the hierarchy of dental and jaw disorders, underscoring its central importance in dental treatment and pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2387
retrieved_docs.content: The phrase “Due to” serves as a causal indicator and is associated with specific injury outcomes, including loss of teeth resulting from an accident (“verlies; tanden door ongeval”) as well as severe trauma such as crushing injuries or internal injuries. Recognizing this wording helps clinicians link the underlying cause to these particular clinical presentations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2732
retrieved_docs.content: Accidental physical contact (event) is described as being due to loss of teeth caused by an accident, indicating that dental injury resulting from an accident can lead to unintended physical contact.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2327
retrieved_docs.content: Application of therapeutic agent and delivery of vertex presentation are both classified as child procedures within the broader category of surgical manipulation, indicating that these specific interventions are considered subtypes of surgical manipulation in medical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2071
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures comprise a range of interventions used to manage complex obstetric conditions and facilitate safe delivery. Among these, the surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancy and abdominal pregnancy represents critical operative approaches for addressing abnormal implantation of the embryo, thereby preventing life‑threatening complications for the mother. In addition, Vaginal Assisted Delivery is included as a procedural option that supports the progression of labor and assists in the birth of the infant when additional support is required. Together, these child procedures under the umbrella of Pregnancy-Related Procedures illustrate the spectrum of surgical and assisted techniques employed in modern obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 1021
retrieved_docs.content: organizes general obstetric procedure categories (إجراء توليدي, Antepartum Procedures, Obstetrics incision) and specific clinical actions—ranging from diagnostic sampling and membrane management to fetal stimulation, reduction of multiple gestations, and surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancies—providing a comprehensive framework for coordinating obstetric care across the ante‑partum, intrapartum, and postpartum periods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 752
retrieved_docs.content: essential for clinicians to anticipate potential delivery complications, decide when normal delivery procedures are feasible, and implement appropriate interventions to optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 4 -- Community 2833
retrieved_docs.content: The category “الإجراءات العلاجية، غير مصنف في موضع آخر” (Therapeutic procedures, not classified elsewhere) includes “جراحة” (Surgery) as a child concept, indicating that surgery is considered a specific type of therapeutic procedure within this broader, uncategorized therapeutic classification.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2430
retrieved_docs.content: Other miscellaneous procedures are classified as a subset of other nonoperative procedures, indicating that they fall under the broader category of nonoperative interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1874
retrieved_docs.content: Porodnické chirurgické výkony, the category of obstetric surgical procedures, includes the specific intervention إجراء تطويق عنق الرحم, a cervical cerclage performed to reinforce the cervical canal and prevent premature cervical dilation, thereby reducing the risk of preterm birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2927
retrieved_docs.content: Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery are classified as a subset of other procedures to assist delivery, indicating that these specific interventions are encompassed within the broader category of techniques used to facilitate and support the childbirth process.
